Stirring a bubbling cauldron with a long wooden spoon, fragrant steam curling around her face
Hanging fresh-cut lavender bundles from ceiling rafters, stretching up on her toes to reach
Grinding dried nettle roots in a stone mortar, pale powder dusting her stained fingers
Pouring shimmering tincture through cheesecloth into amber glass bottles, drops catching lamplight
Kneeling in her garden pulling chickweed from between rows of foxglove and feverfew
Tracing faded handwriting in her grandmother's recipe book, candlelight flickering across cracked pages
Feeding three calico cats on the doorstep, pouring milk into a chipped wooden bowl
Harvesting oyster mushrooms from a mossy log, wicker basket looped over her elbow
Bottling wildflower honey from garden hives, golden streams threading into waiting jars
Holding a potion vial to the window, examining the swirling violet color against sunlight
Braiding dried yarrow and sage into protective wreaths at her cluttered worktable
Carrying split firewood through the cottage door, tabby cat winding between her ankles
Straining elderberry syrup through muslin cloth, dark juice pooling in a ceramic bowl
Tying twine around bunches of rosemary, hanging each bundle from iron hooks overhead
Peeling bark from willow branches with her thumbnail, collecting strips in her apron
Ladling soup into clay bowls for evening meal, hearth fire warming her back
Sweeping dried petals and herb scraps from the floorboards with a bundled twig broom
Wrapping fresh poultices in linen squares, fingers pressing the herb paste firmly inside
Collecting rainwater from barrel spouts into glass jugs, storm clouds darkening the garden
Turning compost behind the cottage with a pitchfork, steam rising from decomposing matter
Trimming candle wicks with small scissors, melted wax pooling in tin holders
Plucking ripe blackberries from thorny brambles, purple stains spreading across her palms
Crushing garlic cloves with the flat of her knife, pungent oil spreading across wood
Scrubbing her iron cauldron with sand and salt, soapy water sloshing over the sides
Shaking seeds from dried poppy heads into an earthenware jar for winter planting
